task:
ATOM 0 — OWNER MAP ONLY (no implementation, no behavior changes). The 'Oz hardening' priority's Objective hard-gates all implementation behind a completed owner map (ADR-0010, durable-orchestration rule). Produce that owner map as a single markdown document at docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md. Read-only against all product code EXCEPT creating that one new doc file.

The owner map MUST concretely name (with real file paths and symbol/function names found by reading the code — not guesses):

1. RUN-STATE PROJECTION SOURCE OF TRUTH. Where does the durable active-run/session/ticket state live that Oz's situational awareness should be a projection of? Identify the file(s)/data structures that hold active run + session state and the ticket state under cocoder/tickets/open/. This is the single engine that Objective items 2 (compact/refresh read path) and 4 (change-detection path) BOTH build on. Name it once.

2. OZ MESSAGE/STATUS RENDER SURFACES. Every surface that renders Oz messages or status: the daemon Oz loop (find it), OzChat.tsx (find its path), and the status feed/projection that Oz reports from. For each: file path, the function/component that owns rendering, and what contract it currently consumes.

3. SETTINGS SURFACE. Where settings are defined/persisted/read, so the new 'Oz Auto Compact at N Runs' field (default 3, range 2-10) has ONE owner, not a parallel toggle. Name the settings type/schema, the persistence layer, and the UI control surface.

4. TESTS/FIXTURES that currently pin each of the above (Oz loop, OzChat rendering, status projection, settings).

5. COORDINATION NOTE for item 1: OzChat.tsx rendering quality (markdown/streaming/thinking) vs the archived workspace-segmentation priority which owned the Oz terminal PANEL LAYOUT and global controls on the same component. State exactly which regions/props each touches so implementation won't clobber the panel work.

6. MAPPING of each Objective item (1 markdown/stream/thinking, 2 compact-on-N-runs, 3 drag-to-ask pointer, 4 auto status pickup) to the specific owner(s) above, plus tickets 0013 and 0015 — say whether item 4 subsumes them.

ACCEPTANCE: docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md exists, every claim cites a real path/symbol you actually read, items 2 & 4 are shown sharing ONE projection engine, and the settings field has a single named owner. MUST NOT: change any product code, alter any behavior, or touch any file other than creating this doc. Report the doc path and a 5-line summary of the source-of-truth you found.

ATOM 1 — Extract the ONE shared Oz awareness projection (consolidation only, NO new features, NO behavior change). The owner map (docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md) found there is no single Oz awareness projection today: packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts factsDigest reads only priorities+runs, and packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts status (runSummary/runsSummary) reads ctx.store directly. Objective items 2 (compact/refresh read) and 4 (change detection) must both build on ONE projection. Build that owner now, with zero observable behavior change.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Create a single daemon-side projection module, e.g. packages/daemon/src/oz-awareness.ts, exporting a pure function that takes the durable read surfaces and returns a structured snapshot of current operating state: active/recent runs (from ctx.store.listRuns({workspaceId})), their key fields, and OPEN tickets (via readTickets(ticketsDir(ws.path)) — the same loader oz-host/routes already use). Keep it a pure read projection: inputs in, snapshot out; no side effects, no compaction logic, no event wiring yet (those are later atoms).
2. Refactor packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts factsDigest to DERIVE its priorities+runs facts from this projection (you may extend the projection to also carry the priorities it already reads, so the digest's current content is reproduced). The Oz prompt's facts output must remain byte-equivalent or trivially equivalent to today for the existing inputs — do NOT add tickets to the digest in this atom (that is item 4's behavior change). Consolidation only.
3. Refactor the oz-chat.ts status read path (runSummary/runsSummary or whatever the status command uses) to read run state through the SAME projection rather than ad-hoc store calls, with identical output.
4. Do NOT change settings, UI, OzChat.tsx, streaming, drag-to-ask, or event emission. Do NOT change the HTTP routes' contracts.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A single new projection function is the lone source both factsDigest and the status path consume for run state.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` (and any core tests you touch) pass; existing oz-chat.test.ts / oz-agent-chat.test.ts / read-surfaces.test.ts stay green with NO assertion changes that weaken them — if a test needs updating, it must be because the seam moved, not because behavior changed.
- `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) is clean.
- Add a focused unit test for the new projection (inputs -> expected snapshot).

MUST NOT: alter Oz's user-visible replies, the facts digest content for existing inputs, settings, or any UI. Report: the new file path, the two call sites you redirected, and the exact test command + result you ran.

ATOM 2 — Objective item 4: Oz picks up newly-committed tickets and run wrap/teardown WITHOUT a manual nudge, via the shared projection from atom 1. Builds on packages/daemon/src/oz-awareness.ts (projectOzAwareness). Owner map: docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md sections 2,4,6. This closes the run_131 symptom (a newly-committed ticket 0014 never appeared in Oz).

SCOPE — two concrete gaps named in the owner map:

A. NEW-TICKET AWARENESS. The owner map found packages/daemon/src/routes.ts createTicket() commits a ticket under cocoder/tickets/open/ but emits NO Oz event, so Oz never re-reads tickets. Fix:
   1. Make the Oz awareness facts digest in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts INCLUDE open tickets from the projection (projectOzAwareness already carries openTickets — surface them in the digest so a status/agent turn reflects them). Add an 'Open tickets (N):' section to factsDigest, formatted like the runs section. This is the intended behavior change deferred from atom 1.
   2. Emit an Oz event when a ticket is created/committed agentically. The owner map names the event surfaces: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (emitOzEvent, attachRunLifecycle) and packages/daemon/src/context.ts (OzEventBus), streamed via routes.ts streamOzEvents / GET /oz/events. Have routes.ts createTicket() emit an Oz event (e.g. 'ticket-created') through the same OzEventBus the run lifecycle uses, so the UI/Oz refetch path (packages/ui/src/main/events-stream.ts startOzEventStream -> App.tsx onOzEvent) refreshes tickets without a manual nudge.

B. WRAP/TEARDOWN PICKUP. Confirm run wrap/teardown already emits an Oz event via attachRunLifecycle/run-settled (owner map says it does). If a completed run's terminal status is NOT already reflected on the next status/refresh because the projection or digest omits it, fix that minimally so the projection's recentRuns/activeRuns reflect the settled run. Do NOT rebuild lifecycle; only ensure the projection + digest surface the post-completion status. If it already works, say so with evidence and make no change there.

ACCEPTANCE:
- factsDigest output now includes an open-tickets section sourced from projectOzAwareness (not a second ticket read).
- createTicket emits an Oz event through the existing OzEventBus, parallel to how run lifecycle emits; no new parallel event contract.
- A test proves: (i) the awareness digest/projection includes a newly-present open ticket, and (ii) createTicket emits the event (assert via the event bus or GET /oz/events test surface, mirroring packages/daemon/tests/events.test.ts).
- The status command after a ticket is added reflects it.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run tests/oz-chat.test.ts tests/oz-agent-chat.test.ts tests/events.test.ts tests/oz-awareness.test.ts` pass (these pin your touched paths). Do NOT weaken assertions. NOTE: tests/mutations.test.ts and tests/authoring-play.test.ts have 5 PRE-EXISTING env-dependent failures (headless run can't spawn -> 'failed' vs 'completed') unrelated to this atom; do not try to fix them here, but do not make them worse.
- Do NOT touch settings, OzChat.tsx rendering, streaming, drag-to-ask, or compaction (later atoms).

Report: the files changed, the exact event name/shape you emitted, the digest section you added, and the test command + result.

ATOM 3 — Add the 'Oz Auto Compact at N Runs' setting to the SINGLE settings owner (settings plumbing only; NO compaction logic yet — that is atom 4). Owner map docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md section 3 and Objective item 2 / resolved design call 2.

The setting: field name ozAutoCompactRuns, type number, DEFAULT 3, valid RANGE 2..10 inclusive. It MUST be daemon-owned (durable), NOT a renderer-only preference (the owner map is explicit: daemon settings persist pollIntervalMs + defaultWorkspaceId; renderer preferences are theme/sound/sendOnEnter/panelRatio and stay local — this new field is daemon-owned and round-trips through daemonPatch).

WHAT TO DO (one owner, align consumers — do not create a parallel toggle):
1. packages/daemon/src/settings.ts: add `ozAutoCompactRuns: number` to the Settings interface, add it to DEFAULT_SETTINGS (value 3), and CLAMP/validate it in saneSettings and sanePatch to the 2..10 range (out-of-range or non-number coerces to the default or nearest valid bound — follow whatever pattern saneSettings already uses for pollIntervalMs; match the existing style). The GET/PUT /settings routes already round-trip the Settings object, so confirm they carry the new field without a separate code path.
2. Renderer bridge: packages/ui/src/main/ipc-contract.ts add ozAutoCompactRuns to the Settings interface + DEFAULT_SETTINGS; packages/ui/src/main/settings-sync.ts (daemonPatch) must include ozAutoCompactRuns in the daemon-owned (NOT renderer-preference) set so it round-trips to the daemon. Mirror in packages/ui/src/renderer/model.ts Settings/DEFAULT_SETTINGS if that is where the renderer reads it.
3. UI control: packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/Settings.tsx — add a numeric field/row (a number input or stepper, range 2..10) labeled like 'Oz Auto Compact at N Runs' wired to saveSettings, following the existing SettingsRow pattern. Keep it consistent with the existing settings form (the app.test.tsx pins that settings render as forms, not raw JSON — do not regress that).

ACCEPTANCE:
- ozAutoCompactRuns exists in exactly the daemon Settings owner and is mirrored through the IPC/renderer bridge with NO second source of truth; default 3; values outside 2..10 are clamped/rejected by saneSettings/sanePatch.
- A daemon test proves the clamp (e.g. sanePatch with 1 -> 2 or default, 11 -> 10 or default, 5 -> 5) and that DEFAULT_SETTINGS.ozAutoCompactRuns === 3. Put it with the existing settings tests (search for where pollIntervalMs sanitization is tested; if none, add a focused settings.test.ts).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run` shows NO NEW failures beyond the known 5 pre-existing env-baseline failures (tests/mutations.test.ts, tests/authoring-play.test.ts — 'failed' vs 'completed'). If a settings test file exists, it passes.
- If you can run the ui settings-sync test: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui exec vitest run tests/settings-sync.test.ts` passes (it pins the daemon/local split — your field must land on the daemon side).
- Do NOT implement compaction, do NOT touch OzChat.tsx rendering, drag-to-ask, or the projection. NOTE: a repo-wide `pnpm -w typecheck` has PRE-EXISTING errors in packages/ui (TS5097 .ts import-extension + a JSON import-attribute) unrelated to this atom; do not chase them, but do not add NEW type errors in the files you touch.

Report: every file changed, the clamp behavior you implemented, the test you added + its result, and confirm the field is daemon-owned (round-trips via daemonPatch), not a renderer-only preference.

ATOM 4 — Objective item 2 core: Oz self-compacts after every N orchestrated runs, reconstructing awareness from durable run state (not chat history). Builds on atom 1 (projectOzAwareness) + atom 3 (ozAutoCompactRuns setting). Owner map docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md sections 1,2 (Headless Oz Agent Loop in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts). Resolved design call 2: runs-count is the PRIMARY trigger; the ~200k token threshold is OUT OF SCOPE for this atom (do not implement it).

KEY INSIGHT (use it): buildPrompt in oz-host.ts ALREADY rebuilds the facts digest from projectOzAwareness every turn, so Oz's operating awareness does NOT live in the transcript — it is reconstructed from durable run/ticket state each turn. Therefore compaction = safely truncating/resetting the OzSession transcript; correctness is preserved by the projection-based digest. Do NOT call an LLM to summarize; that is unnecessary given the projection.

WHAT TO DO (in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts unless noted):
1. Read the threshold N from settings: use readSettings(ctx.cocoderHome).ozAutoCompactRuns (the daemon-owned setting from atom 3; default 3, range 2..10 already clamped). Read it where the turn is handled (tryHandleOzAgentTurn / runToolLoop), not hardcoded.
2. Track orchestrated-run count per OzSession: add a counter to OzSession that increments once per ORCHESTRATED RUN Oz handles. The natural boundary is a settled-run handoff turn — i.e. when tryHandleOzAgentTurn processes an input representing a completed/settled run result (input.kind === 'result' or the equivalent run-completion turn), increment the counter. (A founder free-form chat turn is NOT an orchestrated run; do not count it.) Confirm the exact input discriminator by reading the code; do not guess.
3. When the counter reaches N, COMPACT this session: reset the transcript to empty (or keep at most the last 1 entry for continuity), and reset the counter to 0. Awareness is rebuilt from projectOzAwareness on the next buildPrompt, so no operating state is lost.
4. Ensure a `status` request AFTER a compaction still returns an accurate, current summary of every active run/session. This should already hold because the status path (oz-chat.ts) reads from the projection, not the transcript — verify it and add coverage; if status depends on transcript anywhere, fix it to read from the projection.

ACCEPTANCE (deterministic, scripted):
- A daemon test (extend packages/daemon/tests/oz-agent-chat.test.ts or a new oz-compaction.test.ts) sets ozAutoCompactRuns LOW (e.g. 2) via settings, drives N settled-run-result turns through tryHandleOzAgentTurn/handleOzMessage, and asserts: (a) the session transcript was compacted (length reset to <= the keep-bound) exactly when the Nth orchestrated run completed, and (b) a subsequent status/agent turn still reports the correct active runs sourced from the projection (drive it with known runs in the store and assert they appear). The counter must NOT compact on plain founder chat turns — assert that too.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run tests/oz-agent-chat.test.ts tests/oz-chat.test.ts tests/oz-awareness.test.ts tests/settings.test.ts` (plus your new test file if any) pass. The full daemon suite must show NO NEW failures beyond the known 5 pre-existing env-baseline failures (tests/mutations.test.ts POST /runs lifecycle + tests/authoring-play.test.ts: 'failed' vs 'completed').
- Do NOT touch OzChat.tsx rendering, drag-to-ask, or the settings UI. Do NOT implement the token-threshold trigger. Keep it to the runs-count trigger on the existing projection.

Report: the exact input discriminator you used to detect an orchestrated-run completion, where you read N, the compaction reset behavior (what you keep), the test file + command + result, and confirm status-after-compaction is projection-sourced.

ATOM 5 — Wire the compaction trigger to the REAL run-completion signal so item 2 fires in the running app (atom 4 built the mechanism but nothing in production drives it). Owner map docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md sections 1,2,4.

PROBLEM (verified): atom 4 added per-session orchestratedRunCount + compactAfterOrchestratedRun in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts, but it only increments when tryHandleOzAgentTurn receives a kind:'result' turn — and NO production code sends one. The only caller (oz-chat.ts:113) passes a founder string. So compaction never fires on a real run. The authoritative signal that an orchestrated run completed is the run-settled event emitted at packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:314 (emitOzEvent(ctx, { type: 'run-settled', runId, workspaceId, status })), where workspaceId is in scope.

WHAT TO DO — make run-settled the SINGLE owner of the orchestrated-run count (durable-orchestration one-owner rule; no double counting):
1. In packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts, export a function e.g. `recordOrchestratedRun(ctx: OzContext, workspaceId: string): Promise<void>` (or sync if you don't need settings async — but N comes from readSettings, so async) that: gets-or-creates that workspace's Oz session (reuse getSession), reads N = (await readSettings(ctx.cocoderHome)).ozAutoCompactRuns, and calls the existing compactAfterOrchestratedRun(session, N). This reuses atom 4's mechanism; do not duplicate the increment/reset logic.
2. Call recordOrchestratedRun from the run-settled point in launcher.ts (right where run-settled is emitted) so every settled orchestrated run with a workspaceId bumps that workspace's Oz session and compacts at N. Guard for missing workspaceId.
3. Eliminate double-counting and dead speculative code: run-settled is now the single counting trigger. Remove the orchestrated-run counting from the tryHandleOzAgentTurn kind:'result' path (the `if (turn.kind === 'result' && result.status < 500) compactAfterOrchestratedRun(...)` call). If the kind:'result' input machinery (OzAgentTurnInput result variant, '## Run result' heading, 'result' transcript role) has NO remaining producer or consumer after this, REMOVE it to keep oz-host lean (the founder-string path stays exactly as before). If you judge the result-rendering useful and want to keep it, keep it INERT (no counting) and say why — but default to removing unused machinery.
4. Compaction must remain transcript-reset only (no LLM); awareness is rebuilt from projectOzAwareness on the next buildPrompt — already true. Status after compaction stays projection-sourced.

ACCEPTANCE (deterministic):
- A daemon test proves the END-TO-END trigger: with ozAutoCompactRuns set low (e.g. 2) and a workspace registered, invoking the run-settled path N times for that workspace (call recordOrchestratedRun directly N times, or drive the launcher settle path) compacts that workspace's Oz session transcript exactly at the Nth settle, and a subsequent status/buildPrompt still reports the active runs from the projection (seed the store with known runs and assert they appear while the transcript shows '- none'). Adapt atom 4's existing compaction test to the run-settled trigger rather than the removed result-turn path — do NOT leave a test asserting the deleted behavior.
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run tests/oz-agent-chat.test.ts tests/oz-chat.test.ts tests/oz-awareness.test.ts tests/settings.test.ts` pass. Full daemon suite shows NO NEW failures beyond the known 5 pre-existing env-baseline failures (tests/mutations.test.ts POST /runs lifecycle + tests/authoring-play.test.ts).
- No double-count: a single settled run increments the count by exactly 1. Do NOT touch OzChat.tsx, drag-to-ask, or the token-threshold trigger.

Report: the exact line where you call recordOrchestratedRun in launcher.ts, whether you removed or kept-inert the result-turn machinery (and why), the adapted test + command + result, and confirm a single settled run counts once.
